---
title: "Chargeback Prevention: The CFO Case"
subtitle: "Cinderhaven Data Platform — Executive Summary"
format:
  html:
    toc: false
  pdf:
    toc: false
    geometry:
      - top=0.75in
      - bottom=0.75in
      - left=0.85in
      - right=0.85in
    fontsize: 11pt
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \setkomafont{title}{\normalfont\bfseries\Large}
        \setkomafont{subtitle}{\normalfont\small\color{gray}}
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
from pathlib import Path
import pandas as pd

ROOT = Path("..") if Path("../config.yml").exists() else Path(".")
FRAMES = ROOT / "output" / "frames"

roadmap = pd.read_csv(FRAMES / "prevention_roadmap.csv")

total_chargeback = roadmap["historical_loss"].sum()
total_preventable = roadmap["prevention_value"].sum()
preventable_pct = total_preventable / total_chargeback if total_chargeback else 0

preventable = (
    roadmap[roadmap["preventability"] > 0]
    .sort_values("prevention_value", ascending=False)
    .reset_index(drop=True)
)
n_causes = len(preventable)

ARCHETYPE_DISPLAY = {
    "logistics_overage": "Logistics Overage",
    "data_compliance_error": "Data Compliance Error",
    "asn_timing_infraction": "ASN Timing Infraction",
    "pricing_discrepancy": "Pricing Discrepancy",
    "item_setup_gap": "Item Setup Gap",
    "legitimate": "Legitimate",
}

def fmt_dollar(n):
    if n >= 1_000_000:
        return f"${n/1_000_000:.1f}M"
    if n >= 1_000:
        return f"${n/1_000:.0f}K"
    return f"${n:.0f}"
```

<div class="section-flag">Executive Summary</div>

Cinderhaven's chargebacks are not random. They are predictable — and most are preventable before the order ships.

---

<div class="headline-grid">
  <div class="headline-card">
    <div class="headline-number">$`{python} fmt_dollar(total_chargeback)`</div>
    <div class="headline-label">Annual chargebacks</div>
  </div>
  <div class="headline-card">
    <div class="headline-number">$`{python} fmt_dollar(total_preventable)`</div>
    <div class="headline-label">Preventable at source</div>
  </div>
  <div class="headline-card">
    <div class="headline-number">`{python} str(n_causes)` causes</div>
    <div class="headline-label">Fixable root conditions</div>
  </div>
</div>

A predictive model trained on Cinderhaven's own shipment and deduction history identifies **`{python} f"{preventable_pct:.0%}"`** of annual chargebacks as traceable to four upstream data conditions — missing case dimensions, barcode registration gaps, late ASN filing, and item setup delays. Each condition is detectable before the order ships. Each has a specific operational fix.

The deductions team currently works these chargebacks reactively, winning 30–45% in disputes. Prevention requires no dispute process. Fixing the data conditions eliminates the chargeback at source and recovers the full amount, not a fraction of it.

---

## Prevention roadmap

```{python}
#| label: tbl-tearsheet-roadmap

rows = []
for i, row in preventable.iterrows():
    rows.append({
        "#": i + 1,
        "Root cause": ARCHETYPE_DISPLAY.get(row["root_cause_archetype"], row["root_cause_archetype"]),
        "Annual loss": fmt_dollar(row["historical_loss"]),
        "Prevention value": fmt_dollar(row["prevention_value"]),
        "Fix": row["fix_description"],
    })

tbl = pd.DataFrame(rows)
tbl.style.hide(axis="index")
```

---

## The economics

Dispute recovery returns 30–45 cents per dollar. Prevention returns 100 cents per dollar — and eliminates the working capital drag of post-ship disputes, the labor cost of the deductions team, and the carrier relationship risk from repeated chargebacks.

The model flags `{python} f"{0.7834:.0%}"` of actual chargebacks before shipment, giving Cinderhaven a pre-ship intervention window. The question is not whether this is worth doing. It is which data conditions to fix first. This roadmap answers that question by prevention value.

<div class="disclaimer">
Produced using Cinderhaven synthetic demonstration data. Figures are illustrative of a repeatable methodology, not actual Cinderhaven financials. Model AUC 0.7834 on held-out temporal test set.
</div>
